mcqs.question: What is the primary goal of steganography?

options.text: To make message content unreadable
To hide the existence of the message itself
To verify the identity of the sender
To ensure message integrity

mcqs.explanation: Steganography aims to conceal the very existence of a secret message within an innocent-looking carrier medium. Unlike cryptography which makes content unreadable, steganography makes the communication invisible.

mcqs.question: Which steganography technique is most commonly used for hiding data in digital images?

options.text: Most Significant Bit (MSB) replacement
Least Significant Bit (LSB) replacement
Frequency domain embedding
Header manipulation

mcqs.explanation: LSB replacement is the most common image steganography technique because changing the least significant bits of pixel values creates minimal visual distortion. A pixel value of 200 (11001000) changed to 201 (11001001) is imperceptible.

mcqs.question: What is the fundamental difference between cryptography and steganography?

options.text: Cryptography uses algorithms while steganography uses physical techniques
Cryptography protects message content while steganography hides message existence
Cryptography is more secure than steganography
Cryptography is older than steganography

mcqs.explanation: Cryptography protects the content of messages (makes them unreadable without the key), while steganography hides the existence of messages within carrier media (makes them invisible to observers).

mcqs.question: What is Steganalysis?

options.text: A method of encrypting steganographic messages
The science of detecting hidden messages in steganographic media
A technique for improving the capacity of steganographic systems
A type of steganography that uses text as the carrier

mcqs.explanation: Steganalysis is the counterpart to steganography. It involves techniques for detecting the presence of hidden messages in carrier media, using statistical analysis, comparison methods, and anomaly detection.

mcqs.question: Why is combining cryptography and steganography considered more secure than using either alone?

options.text: It doubles the encryption strength
It provides defense in depth: hiding existence AND protecting content
It eliminates the need for key distribution
It makes brute-force attacks impossible

mcqs.explanation: The combination provides two layers of security: steganography hides the fact that a secret message exists, while cryptography ensures that even if the hidden message is discovered, its content remains unreadable without the key.

mcqs.question: What is the trade-off between capacity and imperceptibility in steganography?

options.text: More capacity always improves imperceptibility
Higher capacity reduces imperceptibility due to more modifications in the carrier
Imperceptibility and capacity are independent of each other
Lower capacity always leads to detection

mcqs.explanation: Hiding more data requires more modifications to the carrier medium, which increases the statistical anomalies and visual/audible artifacts, making the stego object more likely to be detected by steganalysis.

mcqs.question: In image steganography, why is LSB modification preferred over MSB modification?

options.text: Because LSB changes are faster to compute
Because changing the LSB causes minimal visual change compared to MSB
Because MSB modification does not work with color images
Because LSB can store more data than MSB

mcqs.explanation: The LSB contributes the least to a pixel's value (a change of 1 in a 0-255 range), so modifying it is virtually imperceptible. Changing the MSB would alter the pixel value by 128, creating obvious visible artifacts.

mcqs.question: Which historical figure is credited with one of the earliest uses of steganography by tattooing a message on a slave's shaved head?

options.text: Julius Caesar
Histiaeus of Miletus
Augustus Kerckhoffs
Leon Battista Alberti

mcqs.explanation: Histiaeus of Miletus (5th century BC) is credited with one of the earliest recorded uses of steganography. He tattooed a secret message on a slave's shaved head and waited for the hair to regrow before sending the slave as a messenger.
